2603.14824.title: Planning as Goal Recognition: Deriving Heuristics from Intention Models - Extended Version
2603.14824.paper_summary: Classical planning aims to find a sequence of actions, a plan, that maps a starting state into one of the goal states. If a trajectory appears to be leading to the goal, should we prioritise exploring it? Seminal work in goal recognition (GR) has defined GR in terms of a classical planning problem, adopting classical solvers and heuristics to recognise plans. We come full circle, and study the adoption and properties of GR-derived heuristics for seeking solutions to classical planning problems. We propose a new framework for assessing goal intention, which informs a new class of efficiently-computable heuristics. As a proof of concept, we derive two such heuristics, and show that they can already yield improvements for top-scoring classical planners. Our work provides foundational knowledge for understanding and deriving probabilistic intention-based heuristics for planning.
2603.14824.paper_summary_zh: 古典規劃旨在找到一系列行動，即計劃，將起始狀態映射到目標狀態之一。<lb/>如果一條軌跡似乎正在通向目標，我們應該優先探索它嗎？<lb/>在目標識別（GR）方面的開創性工作已將GR定義為一個古典規劃問題，採用古典求解器和啟發式方法來識別計劃。<lb/>我們回到起點，研究GR衍生的啟發式方法在尋求古典規劃問題解決方案中的採用和特性。<lb/>我們提出了一個新的框架來評估目標意圖，這為一類高效可計算的啟發式方法提供了依據。<lb/>作為概念驗證，我們推導出兩種此類啟發式方法，並顯示它們已經能為得分最高的古典規劃者帶來改進。<lb/>我們的工作為理解和推導基於概率的意圖啟發式方法在規劃中的應用提供了基礎知識。
2603.14824.author: Giacomo Rosa et.al.
2603.14824.authors: Giacomo Rosa, Jean Honorio, Nir Lipovetzky, Sebastian Sardina
